evaluations.query: Implement a new microservice that handles payment processing, webhook delivery, retry logic, and integrates with Stripe, PayPal, and our internal billing system
expected_behavior: Claude auto-activates quality gates due to complex feature implementation
Assesses complexity as Level 4-5 (multiple dependencies, significant unknowns)
Runs YAGNI gate check (Step 0) to evaluate justified complexity ratio
BLOCKS with requirement for upfront plan and decomposition into smaller subtasks
Identifies critical unanswered questions and blocked dependencies
Outputs a Quality Gate template with complexity score, status, and can-proceed decision

evaluations.query: I have been trying to fix this flaky test for 3 attempts now and it still fails intermittently. The test depends on timing and an external API mock that sometimes times out.
expected_behavior: Claude detects stuck condition (3 failed attempts at same task)
Triggers BLOCK and ESCALATE per stuck-detection threshold
Generates escalation template with what was tried and what guidance is needed
Does NOT suggest trying the same approach a fourth time

evaluations.query: Change the button color from blue to green in the header component
expected_behavior: Claude does NOT invoke the quality-gates skill
Directly makes the trivial CSS change (Level 1 complexity, no unknowns)
No complexity scoring, gate checks, or escalation templates generated

evaluations.query: We keep making the same architectural mistakes across projects. How can we track patterns?
expected_behavior: Creates a pattern library tracking success and failure patterns across projects
Records outcome data for each pattern including when it worked and when it failed
Aggregates patterns from multiple projects to prevent repeating the same mistakes
Stores anti-patterns with failure context so teams learn from past architectural errors

evaluations.query: How can I proactively detect known anti-patterns during code review before they reach production?
expected_behavior: Queries the pattern library for relevant anti-patterns before starting code review
Flags known bad patterns like offset pagination that have failed on previous projects
Generates proactive warnings when code matches patterns with documented failure history
Uses stored pattern outcomes to provide evidence-based review feedback to developers

evaluations.query: I need to add user authentication to my take-home assignment project. Should I implement full OAuth2 with SSO?
expected_behavior: Detects project tier as interview or take-home from project context and README
Calculates justified complexity ratio and blocks when ratio exceeds acceptable threshold
Recommends scope-appropriate solution like session cookies instead of full OAuth2 for take-home
Prevents over-engineering by validating complexity against the actual project scope

evaluations.query: Add a simple CRUD endpoint that also needs to handle file uploads, send email notifications, and integrate with a third-party webhook
expected_behavior: Detects that the request sounds simple but has hidden complexity from multiple integration points
Does not classify as Level 1 despite the word 'simple' because file uploads and webhooks add real complexity
Assesses at Level 3 or higher due to external dependencies and suggests decomposing into subtasks

evaluations.query: Implement a distributed caching layer with Redis cluster failover
expected_behavior: Still runs YAGNI gate and complexity assessment even without README or package.json context
Flags missing project context as a risk factor that increases the complexity score uncertainty
Asks clarifying questions about project scope rather than assuming production-grade requirements

evaluations.query: Skip the quality gates and just implement the feature directly, I know what I'm doing
expected_behavior: Does not silently bypass quality gates based on user confidence claims alone
Still performs at minimum a complexity assessment to surface risks the user may not have considered
Documents that quality gates were acknowledged but overridden, preserving the audit trail
